title: Projects
text: This page showcases some of my International Business and Marketing group projects and assignments that I have completed as a student at Cal Poly Pomona.

title: Projects
text:
Market Analysis - Functional Programming assignment
In this Introduction to Functional Programming in R assignment, I learned how to create functions. The benefits of functions are that you dont have to copy and paste the code mutiple times, especially when only a single field like “year” has to be changed.

Created functions
Added visualizations

title: Projects
text:
Consumer Behavior - Repositioned Airbnb
As part of a group project for Consumer Behavior course, we focused on repositioning Airbnb to a new customer segment. I researched the company market analysis and also created a perceptual map.
As a group:

Decided to reposition Airbnb from current customer segment, vacation rentals, to digital nomads and remote workers.

My part:

Researched corporate history, brand values, products, state of their business, and price, promotion and distribution
Analyzed their competitors and also created a perceptual map in which I positioned all on a two axis graph, low cost - high cost and low work suitability - high work suitability

title: Projects
section: International Logistics - Export DAP fertilizer to SE Asia
text:
International Logistics - Export DAP fertilizer to SE Asia
As part of a final group project for International Logistics course, we focused on exporting DAP fertilizer to Southeast Asia, specifically Thailand, Indonesia and the Philippines. I researched the International Methods of Market Entry, Logistics Infrastructure, International Methods of Payment and Incoterms (International Commercial Terms) for Indonesia.
As a group:

Collaborated with group members to develop an entry point strategy from the United States into the region.
Established specific criteria to keep in mind, most cost effective option and customer responsiveness.
Determined exporting the ferilizer as bulk, in the ships cargo hold, to Thailand, as the regional distributor, was the right strategy for us.

My part:

Analyzed the Indonesia Country Commercial Guide by the International Trade Administration U.S. Department of Commerce to seek guidance from professionals.

Focused on:

Logistics Infrastructure:

Ports, airports, and intermodal hubs
Customs procedures and regulatory considerations
Role of free trade zones and special economic zones

International Methods of Payment

Letters of credit, documentary collections, open account, advance payment
Comparison of risk levels for each method
Best practices for securing international transactions

Incoterms (International Commercial Terms)

Explanation of key Incoterms (EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP, etc.)
Responsibilities of buyers and sellers under each term
Choosing the right Incoterm for different scenarios


As a result, I chose the Port of Tanjung Priok in Jakarta as the entry point to Indonesia from Thailand. This port is the largest and most advanced in Indonesia.

title: Hi, I’m Alejandro Camara
text: Hi, I’m Alejandro Camara! I’m a fourth-year Business Administration major at Cal Poly Pomona with concentrations in International Business and Marketing Management, graduating in December 2026.

text: Hi, I’m Alejandro Camara! I’m a fourth-year Business Administration major at Cal Poly Pomona with concentrations in International Business and Marketing Management, graduating in December 2026.

title: Hi, I’m Alejandro Camara
text:
Personal Statement
As an International Business and Marketing Management student, I am passionate about logistics, data analysis, and using insights to support better business decisions. My strengths include critical thinking, time management, and decision making. Throughout my academic journey, I have developed technical skills in Excel and R, including data wrangling and data visualization.
Before graduating, I aim to secure an internship where I can gain hands-on experience and expand my skill set. My short-term goal is to transition into a full-time position where I can continue to grow and become a valuable contributor.
